old_claim.note: Clinical Medicine provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: BIOS-3 is directly characterized by bioregenerative closed-system research; clinical medicine is not a distinctive causal prerequisite for the scoped milestone.

source_shape.source_locator: NASA's record describes the Krasnoyarsk Bios-3 facility, Chlorella and higher-plant gas exchange, long-duration human habitation, and closed-system research.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The BIOS-3 review identifies the facility, plant and algal gas exchange, controlled ecology, and long-duration human habitation as the defining system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The system account supplies specific biological and space-life-support lineage while providing no basis for elevating clinical medicine into a direct graph dependency.

ontology_before: The BIOS-3 node inherited a generic clinical medicine edge from a broad placeholder life-support scope.
ontology_after: The BIOS-3 node retains only the earlier capabilities tied to closed-system chemistry, filtration lineage, and space-life-support research.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-launch-source-check-completion: closed_loop_life_support_early_forms no longer directly depends on medicine_clinical.
would_reject_if: A system-specific source demonstrates that clinical medicine was indispensable to the 1972 BIOS-3 design.
The node is broadened back to every subsystem used in all confined habitats.
short_reason: BIOS-3 is directly characterized by bioregenerative closed-system research; clinical medicine is not a distinctive causal prerequisite for the scoped milestone.
